Klasse CipherDataService

java.lang.Object
de.fhdwhannover.integrea.service.csv.CipherDataService
Alle implementierten Schnittstellen:
DataService<Character>
public class CipherDataService extends Object implements DataService<Character>
Processes CSV-based cipher data and converts it into a population of Individual objects for use in evolutionary algorithms.

This service reads raw CSV rows—typically representing encoded character sequences—and transforms each row into an Individual consisting of one or more Chromosome instances. Each non-empty cell in a CSV row is interpreted as a sequence of characters, which becomes a chromosome.

The service is designed for cryptanalysis scenarios where the initial population is derived from cipher-related input data. It also stores the original ciphertext so that downstream components (e.g., fitness functions) can access it.

  • Konstruktordetails

    • CipherDataService

      public CipherDataService(CsvReader csvReader, String cipherText)
      Creates a new cipher data service.
      Parameter:
      csvReader - the CSV reader used to load raw input data
      cipherText - the ciphertext associated with the dataset

  • Methodendetails

    • processData

      public List<Individual<Character>> processData(List<String[]> rawData)
      Converts raw CSV rows into a list of Individual objects.

      Each row is interpreted as a set of chromosomes. Every non-empty cell becomes a chromosome whose gene sequence is derived from the characters of the cell's string value.

      Angegeben von:
      processData in Schnittstelle DataService<Character>
      Parameter:
      rawData - the raw CSV data, where each array represents a row
      Gibt zurück:
      a list of individuals constructed from the CSV content

    • getCipherText

      public String getCipherText()
      Returns the ciphertext associated with this dataset.
      Gibt zurück:
      the original ciphertext
